and **Larry Nicolas Bourgeois** (born 6 December 1988), professionally known as **Les Twins**, are French dancers, [choreographers](/wiki/Choreography "Choreography"), producers, models, designers, and creative directors of their brand "Eleven Paris". Often referred to by their respective nicknames, "Lil Beast" and "Ca Blaze", they are recognized internationally for their talents in [new style hip-hop](/wiki/Hip-hop_dance#Commercial/New_Style "Hip-hop dance") dancing, and various dancing styles.

**Laurent** and **Larry Nicolas Bourgeois** (born 6 December 1988), professionally known as **Les Twins**, are French dancers, [choreographers](/wiki/Choreography "Choreography"), producers, models, designers, and creative directors of their brand "Eleven Paris". Often referred to by their respective nicknames, "Lil Beast" and "Ca Blaze", they are recognized internationally for their talents in [new style hip-hop](/wiki/Hip-hop_dance#Commercial/New_Style "Hip-hop dance") dancing, and various dancing styles.

Born and raised in [Sarcelles](/wiki/Sarcelles "Sarcelles"), [France](/wiki/France "France"), the self-taught dance duo became the favorites of the French audience in 2008 as finalists on the popular television show *[Incroyable Talent](/wiki/Incroyable_Talent "Incroyable Talent")*. They quickly rose to prominence in the United States after a video of their performance on the [San Diego](/wiki/San_Diego "San Diego") leg of the 2010 [World of Dance](/wiki/World_of_Dance "World of Dance") tour went viral on YouTube, with over 46 million views as of 1 December 2019[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Les_Twins&action=edit). In 2011, they won the hip-hop new style division of the prestigious international street dance competition Juste Debout. In 2017, they won the American [reality competition](/wiki/Reality_competition "Reality competition") series *[World of Dance](/wiki/World_of_Dance_(TV_series) "World of Dance (TV series)")*, executively produced by [Jennifer Lopez](/wiki/Jennifer_Lopez "Jennifer Lopez").[[1]](#cite_note-auto-1) The series featured solo acts and larger groups, competing for a grand prize of US$1 million.[[1]](#cite_note-auto-1)

Les Twins have been featured dancers for various music artists, including, [Jason Derulo](/wiki/Jason_Derulo "Jason Derulo"), [Big Sean](/wiki/Big_Sean "Big Sean"), [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé"), [Meghan Trainor](/wiki/Meghan_Trainor "Meghan Trainor"), and [Missy Elliott](/wiki/Missy_Elliott "Missy Elliott"). Standing at a towering height of 6'4" (193 cm) tall, the brothers have modeled for French [haute couture](/wiki/Haute_couture "Haute couture") fashion designer [Jean Paul Gaultier](/wiki/Jean_Paul_Gaultier "Jean Paul Gaultier") and appeared in many high-profile, commercial advertising campaigns, such as [YSL](/wiki/Yves_Saint_Laurent_(fashion_house) "Yves Saint Laurent (fashion house)"), [Chanel](/wiki/Chanel "Chanel"), [Gucci](/wiki/Gucci "Gucci"), [Prada](/wiki/Prada "Prada"), [Versace](/wiki/Versace "Versace"), [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z")'s [Rocawear](/wiki/Rocawear "Rocawear"), [Givenchy](/wiki/Givenchy "Givenchy"), [Vouge](/wiki/Vouge "Vouge"), [Emporio Armani](/wiki/Emporio_Armani "Emporio Armani"), [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan "Jordan") and [Beats by Dre](/wiki/Beats_by_Dre "Beats by Dre"). They have also walked for many shows before becoming celebrities. In 2019 Les Twins also signed with The Jordan Family, where they collaborated on the Air Jordan 1 Retro High *Les Twin – Fearless*.

In addition to teaching their original choreography in workshops around the globe, they are also the founding members of the successful dance team Criminalz Crew and founding members of "Eleven Paris".

## Early life and career beginnings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Les_Twins&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and career beginnings")]

The twins were born on 6 December 1988, in [Sarcelles](/wiki/Sarcelles "Sarcelles"), a commune in the northern projects of [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), [France](/wiki/France "France"). They are the youngest in a large, [Guadeloupean](/wiki/Guadeloupe "Guadeloupe")[[2]](#cite_note-NomadikNation-2) extended family of 9 children.[[3]](#cite_note-BornToDance-3)

Neither Larry nor Laurent received any formal dance training. Instead, they learned by observing other [breakers](/wiki/B-boying "B-boying"), [lockers](/wiki/Locking_(dance) "Locking (dance)"), and [poppers](/wiki/Popping "Popping") and by teaching themselves classical [ballet](/wiki/Ballet "Ballet") techniques.[[4]](#cite_note-BagsyTV-4)[[5]](#cite_note-4Dance.by-5)[[6]](#cite_note-DanceDelightMag-6) At the age of 12, they began performing in concerts and musicals. According to WAD Magazine, "They were born dancers. They walked at 5 months, seriously moved their bodies at 18 months old, and at 9 they showed off steps they had invented to youngsters twice their age in their neighborhood."[[7]](#cite_note-WADMag-7)

In 2005, the brothers recruited five members to form their own dance team called Criminalz Crew, spending the next few years competing in hip-hop tournaments and entertaining crowds as street performers and in clubs.[[3]](#cite_note-BornToDance-3) In 2007, they danced to sold-out audiences in the musical *Pas de Quartier* at France's esteemed [Avignon Festival](/wiki/Festival_d%E2%80%99Avignon "Festival d’Avignon").[[8]](#cite_note-VogueItalia-8)[[9]](#cite_note-PasdeQuartier-9)

## Dance career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Les_Twins&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Dance career")]

In March 2008, Les Twins gained the attention of the dance community by defeating French street dancers Joseph Go and Meech Onomo at the semi-finals of the prestigious international dance competition Juste Debout.[[10]](#cite_note-LTBio_SDKEurope-10) Following that success, they were finalists and audience favorites on Season 3 of *[Incroyable Talent](/wiki/La_France_a_un_incroyable_talent "La France a un incroyable talent")* (the French equivalent of [Britain's Got Talent](/wiki/Britain%27s_Got_Talent "Britain's Got Talent")) aired on [M6](/wiki/M6_(TV_channel) "M6 (TV channel)").[[11]](#cite_note-IncroyableTalent-11) On 13 November 2008, the twins were among the top twelve finalists competing on the Incroyable Talent finale show attended by [Princess Stéphanie of Monaco](/wiki/Princess_St%C3%A9phanie_of_Monaco "Princess Stéphanie of Monaco") and watched by over 4 million viewers.[[12]](#cite_note-IncroyableTalent_finale-12) In 2009, they were contracted to perform the two-man hip-hop comedy stage revue *Twins* choreographed by their long-time coach Abibou "Playmo" Kébe at the Théâtre Trévise in Paris.[[13]](#cite_note-Twins_Trevise-13)

In August 2010, a video of their performance on the [World of Dance](/wiki/World_of_Dance "World of Dance") tour in [San Diego](/wiki/San_Diego "San Diego") went viral on YouTube.[[14]](#cite_note-WOD_SanDiego2010-14) On 9 November 2010, they made their U.S. television debut on [The Ellen DeGeneres Show](/wiki/The_Ellen_DeGeneres_Show "The Ellen DeGeneres Show").[[15]](#cite_note-Ellen1-15)[[16]](#cite_note-Ellen2-16)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cb/Les_Twins_performing_at_Beyonce_Revel_concert_2012-05-28.png/250px-Les_Twins_performing_at_Beyonce_Revel_concert_2012-05-28.png)](/wiki/File:Les_Twins_performing_at_Beyonce_Revel_concert_2012-05-28.png)

Les Twins performing at the [Revel Presents: Beyoncé Live](/wiki/Revel_Presents:_Beyonc%C3%A9_Live "Revel Presents: Beyoncé Live") concert in Atlantic City on 28 May 2012

In March 2011, the twins won the Hip-hop New Style division at Juste Debout, beating out over 353 dancers.[[3]](#cite_note-BornToDance-3) That same year they performed alongside [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé") at the [2011](/wiki/2011_Billboard_Music_Awards "2011 Billboard Music Awards") [*Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Award "Billboard Music Award"), the U.K.'s [Glastonbury Festival](/wiki/Glastonbury_Festival_2011 "Glastonbury Festival 2011"), the [BET Awards](/wiki/2011_BET_Awards "2011 BET Awards"), and the [Good Morning America](/wiki/Good_Morning_America "Good Morning America") Summer Concert Series, garnering even greater attention on the world stage.[[17]](#cite_note-BETAwards_2011-17) They also joined her on the French television shows [X-Factor](/wiki/X_Factor_(France) "X Factor (France)") and [Le Grand Journal](/wiki/Le_Grand_Journal_(Canal%2B) "Le Grand Journal (Canal+)") to promote her then upcoming album *[4](/wiki/4_(Beyonc%C3%A9_album)#Promotion "4 (Beyoncé album)")*, and were the only featured male dancers in the alternate version of her "[Run the World (Girls)](/wiki/Run_the_World_(Girls)#Background_and_development "Run the World (Girls)")" music video.[[18]](#cite_note-MTVNews_RunTheWorld-18) Their collaboration with Knowles would continue in May 2012 at the [Revel Presents: Beyoncé Live](/wiki/Revel_Presents:_Beyonc%C3%A9_Live "Revel Presents: Beyoncé Live") concert series in [Atlantic City](/wiki/Atlantic_City,_New_Jersey "Atlantic City, New Jersey").[[19]](#cite_note-Beyonce_Revel-19)

Larry and Laurent were star dancers on the [Michael Jackson: The Immortal World Tour](/wiki/Michael_Jackson:_The_Immortal_World_Tour "Michael Jackson: The Immortal World Tour") by [Cirque du Soleil](/wiki/Cirque_du_Soleil "Cirque du Soleil") until December 2011.[[20]](#cite_note-Cirque_vid-20)[[21]](#cite_note-Cirque_End-21)

On 6 March 2012, the pair joined rapper [Big Sean](/wiki/Big_Sean "Big Sean") on-stage at the after-party for [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West")'s Fall 2012 fashion show at La Halle Freyssinet in Paris.[[22]](#cite_note-BigSean_KanyeAfterparty-22) One month later, they performed with [Missy Elliott](/wiki/Missy_Elliott "Missy Elliott") and [Timbaland](/wiki/Timbaland "Timbaland") at [Hennessy](/wiki/Hennessy "Hennessy")'s "Wild Rabbit" campaign launch event in New York.[[23]](#cite_note-MissyNYC-23)

Les Twins debuted on Japan's largest television network [Nippon TV](/wiki/Nippon_TV "Nippon TV") on 24 July 2012.[[24]](#cite_note-NipponTV-_StarDraft1-24)

During November 2012, they guest starred on the television dance competition [Everybody Dance!](/wiki/Everybody_Dance!_(TV_series) "Everybody Dance! (TV series)")[[25]](#cite_note-EverybodyDance1-25) in [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine") and teamed up again with [Missy Elliott](/wiki/Missy_Elliott "Missy Elliott") at the *Beats on the Beach* event in [Abu Dhabi](/wiki/Abu_Dhabi "Abu Dhabi") and the *Back2Black Festival* in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil").[[26]](#cite_note-Missy_AbuDhabi-26)[[27]](#cite_note-Missy_Back2Black-27)

In December 2012, the twins began working on multiple projects with [Sony Japan](/wiki/Sony_Music_Entertainment_Japan "Sony Music Entertainment Japan").[[28]](#cite_note-LTofficial_Japan-28) In 2013, they toured with Beyoncé as the only male dancers on [The Mrs. Carter Show World Tour](/wiki/The_Mrs._Carter_Show_World_Tour "The Mrs. Carter Show World Tour").[[29]](#cite_note-:0-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30) The duo was also featured in the music video of "[Blow](/wiki/Blow_(Beyonc%C3%A9_song) "Blow (Beyoncé song)")" and "[Jealous](/wiki/Jealous_(Beyonc%C3%A9_song) "Jealous (Beyoncé song)")" from Beyoncé's [self-titled fifth studio album](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9_(album) "Beyoncé (album)").

In 2012 and 2014, Les Twins appeared in episodes of Avengers of eXtreme Illusions, the revolutionary dance series by [Welling Films](/wiki/Welling_Films "Welling Films"). After the videos came out, Les Twins put their own cut of Welling's work on their own YouTube channel.

In 2014, Les Twins joined Beyoncé and her husband [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") on their [On the Run Tour](/wiki/On_the_Run_Tour_(Beyonc%C3%A9_and_Jay-Z) "On the Run Tour (Beyoncé and Jay-Z)"). In 2015, Les Twins appeared in a special freestyle dance in the song "Tattoo" in Bollywood film [ABCD 2](/wiki/ABCD_2 "ABCD 2"),[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32) which was directed by Remo D'Souza. They were featured in [Beyoncé's 2018 Coachella performance](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9_2018_Coachella_performance "Beyoncé 2018 Coachella performance").

In 2023, Les Twins accompanied Jason Derulo on the "Friday Livez" tour.

In 2023, they joined Beyoncé on the European and North American legs of her [Renaissance World Tour](/wiki/Renaissance_World_Tour "Renaissance World Tour").

In 2024 Les Twins joined Jason Derulo on both his Nu King World Tour and his Vegas Residency.

In 2025, they joined Beyoncé during the European shows of her [Cowboy Carter Tour](/wiki/Cowboy_Carter_Tour "Cowboy Carter Tour").

## Modeling

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Les_Twins&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Modeling")]

Les Twins are signed with [NEXT Model Management](/wiki/NEXT_Model_Management "NEXT Model Management") in Paris.[[33]](#cite_note-NextModelParis-33) In 2010, they walked the runway at [Paris Fashion Week](/wiki/Paris_Fashion_Week "Paris Fashion Week") for the Jean Paul Gaultier Fall 2010 Men's Collection.[[34]](#cite_note-Gaultier_2010-34)

They have since been featured in numerous web, television, and print advertising campaigns, including for [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z")'s viral video campaign ["That's Rocawear"](/wiki/Rocawear "Rocawear"), [New Era](/wiki/New_Era_Cap_Company "New Era Cap Company"), [Benetton](/wiki/Benetton_Group "Benetton Group"), [Givenchy](/wiki/Givenchy "Givenchy"), [H&M](/wiki/H%26M "H&M"), [Adidas](/wiki/Adidas "Adidas"), [Pioneer Steez](/wiki/Pioneer_Corporation "Pioneer Corporation"), [EMOBILE](/wiki/EMOBILE "EMOBILE"), [Emporio Armani](/wiki/Emporio_Armani "Emporio Armani"), [Hogan](/wiki/Hogan "Hogan"), [Vouge](/wiki/Vouge "Vouge"), [Hennessy](/wiki/Hennessy "Hennessy"), [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan "Jordan"), [25 Magazine](/wiki/25_Magazine "25 Magazine"), [Beats By Dre](/wiki/Beats_Electronics "Beats Electronics"), and [Hewlett-Packard](/wiki/Hewlett-Packard "Hewlett-Packard").[[35]](#cite_note-Modeling_AdCampaigns-35)

## Choreography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Les_Twins&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Choreography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d0/Les_Twins_HHI_workshop_2012-08-05.png/250px-Les_Twins_HHI_workshop_2012-08-05.png)](/wiki/File:Les_Twins_HHI_workshop_2012-08-05.png)

Les Twins teaching at the [HHI Urban Moves Workshop](/wiki/Hip_Hop_International "Hip Hop International") in Las Vegas on 5 August 2012

Larry and Laurent have traveled the globe teaching their original choreography in countries including France, the U.S, Japan, Canada, Russia, Germany, Italy, Finland, Ukraine, Poland, Hungary, the Czech Republic, and Kyrgyzstan. Crowds of up to 5000 people have been in attendance at some of their international dance workshops.[[7]](#cite_note-WADMag-7)

In 2010, they choreographed the dance sequence to the song "[Jump](/wiki/Jump_(Kris_Kross_song) "Jump (Kris Kross song)")" by [Kris Kross](/wiki/Kris_Kross "Kris Kross") in the [Wii](/wiki/Wii "Wii") video game *[Just Dance 2](/wiki/Just_Dance_2 "Just Dance 2")*.[[36]](#cite_note-JustDance-36)

In June 2011, [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") broadcast a portion of the audition piece of contestants D\*Day (Damon Bellmon and Deon Lewis) on Season 8 of the dance competition [So You Think You Can Dance](/wiki/So_You_Think_You_Can_Dance "So You Think You Can Dance").[[37]](#cite_note-SYTYCD_contro1-37) Although they were accused by many of plagiarizing their choreography from Les Twins' World of Dance San Diego performance on YouTube, D\*Day was allowed to proceed to the next stage of the competition.[[38]](#cite_note-SYTYCD_contro2-38) In 2012, Bellman and Lewis returned on the *[So You Think You Can Dance](/wiki/So_You_Think_You_Can_Dance "So You Think You Can Dance")* Season 9 Atlanta auditions show claiming (with archived footage from the Show) their prior year's routine was a homage to Les Twins’ work.[[39]](#cite_note-SYTYCD_contro3-39)[[40]](#cite_note-SYTYCD_contro4-40)

In 2018 Laurent helped choreograph for Luka and Jenalyn for their performance in the 2018 Blackpool Music Festival.[[29]](#cite_note-:0-29)

In 2024 Les Twins Choreographed the music video for KING & Jason Derulo - Bumpa

## Artistry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Les_Twins&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Artistry")]

### Dance style

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Les_Twins&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Dance style")]

Les Twins’ dance style is often categorized as [New Style hip-hop](/wiki/Hip-hop_dance#Commercial/New_Style "Hip-hop dance"), a commercial form of [hip hop](/wiki/Hip-hop_dance "Hip-hop dance") with a strong emphasis on isolations and musicality.[[41]](#cite_note-41) However, the twins are known for having created a unique style from this dance which they refer to as "Twins-Style".[[42]](#cite_note-SDMMag-42) Due to their ability to anticipate and finish each other's moves,[[43]](#cite_note-WOD_LTbio-43) the brothers quickly gained a reputation for their timing and skill at making freestyle dance look like choreography.[[3]](#cite_note-BornToDance-3) Charlotte Guillemin of *WAD Magazine* wrote:[[7]](#cite_note-WADMag-7)

> They have really created a form of hip-hop that is truly individual to them...They are in perfect symbiosis. They look totally disjointed with their very jerky movements, although at the same time displaying an amazing fluidity. They undulate, bend and twist, impossible to assess where their center of gravity lies: both phenomenal and unreal.

### Influences

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Les_Twins&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Influences")]

Larry and Laurent credit their major inspirations as [Jim Carrey](/wiki/Jim_Carrey "Jim Carrey"), [Jackie Chan](/wiki/Jackie_Chan "Jackie Chan"), [Bruce Lee](/wiki/Bruce_Lee "Bruce Lee"), [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson"), their coach Abibou "Playmo" Kébe, cartoons, and their own siblings, all of whom are also dancers.[[3]](#cite_note-BornToDance-3)[[6]](#cite_note-DanceDelightMag-6)[[44]](#cite_note-Influences_Interview-44)

### Public image

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Les_Twins&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Public image")]

Les Twins are immediately recognizable for their large [afros](/wiki/Afro "Afro") and trademark style of dress—backwards pants, neck warmers, and kneepads worn around the ankles.[[7]](#cite_note-WADMag-7)[[8]](#cite_note-VogueItalia-8) Regarding their tendency to adopt other unique accessories, such as beaded bracelets and necklaces, wristbands, watch chains, scarves, and ladies’ wear, Laurent stated: "We have changed consciousness about fashion."[[45]](#cite_note-WoofinMag-45)
